description: Thick cord made by twisting fibers together; strong flexible line for pulling or binding. 'Suro' names what connects with strength—fibers twisted into reliable bond.
sound_symbolism: The word coils strong. 'Su' slides continuous in length, rope extending. Then 'ro' coils round, strength through twisting.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'mia roe suro wiru kolua.' (I carry the basket with the rope.) Stronger than 'sima' (thread) and 'nira' (cord)—for pulling, climbing, securing. Multiple strands twisted together.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Connection through strength—many strands twisted together become unbreakable bond.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Rope enabled work—sailing, building, farming, the strong line that pulls and secures.
